# 🚢 Titanic Survival Classification Project

## 📌 Overview
This project is part of a Machine Learning assignment that involves building both regression and classification models.

This section focuses on the **classification task** using the Titanic dataset to predict whether a passenger survived or not.

---

## 🎯 Objective
To build a Logistic Regression model that predicts passenger survival based on features such as age, sex, passenger class, fare, and embarkation point.

---

## 📁 Project Structure

classification_titanic/
│
├── data/
│   └── train.csv
│
├── notebook/
│   └── classification_titanic.ipynb
│
├── outputs/
│   ├── plots/
│   └── models/
│
├── README.md
└── main_README.md

---

## 🔄 Workflow

1. Load Dataset  
2. Data Cleaning  
3. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)  
4. Feature Engineering  
5. Train/Test Split  
6. Model Training (Logistic Regression)  
7. Predictions  
8. Evaluation (Confusion Matrix & ROC Curve)

---

## 📊 Model Used
- Logistic Regression (Supervised Learning Algorithm)

---

## 📈 Evaluation Metrics
- Accuracy Score  
- Confusion Matrix  
- ROC Curve (AUC Score)

---

## 🧠 Key Insights
- Female passengers had higher survival rates
- Passenger class strongly influenced survival probability
- Fare is positively correlated with survival

---

## 🚀 Technologies Used
- Python
- Pandas
- NumPy
- Matplotlib
- Seaborn
- Scikit-learn
